title: 10. Sensory Sources
description: The core mental model for Background Awareness, source tabs, optional reactions, and safe background review behavior.

steps.title: What Sensory Sources Are
steps.body: Sensory sources let NC notice context outside the chat box. Screen, Clipboard, Webcam, and Heart Rate are optional inputs for Background Awareness, and each source can stay off until you want it. Use Read steps aloud or Read Step in this tutorial panel if you want spoken guidance while you work.

steps.title: Open Host -> Vision
steps.body: Vision is the host page for Background Awareness. It controls which sources are available, whether NC may review them quietly, and whether background results may speak or request images.

steps.title: Included Sources
steps.body: The source selector decides which source cards are active. If a source tutorial mentions a missing tab, add that source here first.

steps.title: Enable Background Review
steps.body: Enable background review when you want NC to quietly check selected sources and keep useful context for later. The old developer term for this loop is hidden PING/PONG, but normal users should think of it as Background Awareness.

steps.title: Speech and Image Policy
steps.body: These policy toggles decide whether background review results may proactively speak or request images. Keep them off for a quieter companion.

steps.title: Source Reaction Cards
steps.body: Source-specific reaction cards attach optional behavior to a source. They should describe what to notice, when to react, and how strong the reaction should be without turning every observation into a visible message.
